data.title: Oregon Community Foundation Sells Over 11,000 Shares of Jewett Cameron Trading Co Ltd
data.description:
Reporter NameOregon Community Foundation
Relationship10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$42,175
SEC FilingForm 4

The Oregon Community Foundation, a 10% owner in Jewett Cameron Trading Co Ltd, has sold a total of 11,234 shares of common stock across two transactions on July 1 and July 2, 2025. The sales were conducted at prices of $3.7556 and $3.7494 per share, respectively, totaling $42,175. Following these transactions, the Oregon Community Foundation directly owns 916,545 shares of Jewett Cameron Trading Co Ltd.

SEC Filing: JEWETT CAMERON TRADING CO LTD [ JCTCF ] - Form 4 - Jul. 03, 2025

data.title: World Trading Tournament Reschedules Main Event to March 2026 and Announces Official Mini Tournament Series
data.description:
HOCKESSIN, Delaware, July 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The World Trading Tournament (WTT) announces a strategic update regarding its operations and event calendar. In line with long-term planning and platform optimization, the WTT Main Event, initially scheduled for July 2025, will now take place in March 2026.

This adjustment reflects the organization’s ongoing commitment to delivering a reliable and inclusive global trading experience. The decision was driven by several key developments:

  • Platform Expansion and Technology Enhancements:WTT is integrating advanced infrastructure to support real-time performance tracking, global participant access, and enhanced security. The goal is to ensure a seamless tournament experience for all users and traders.
  • Strategic Partnerships:Several institutional and fintech partners have expressed interest in deeper collaboration. Extending the timeline allows WTT to onboard these partners more effectively, ensuring alignment across key objectives.
  • Global Outreach:This strategic shift enables WTT to expand its global marketing and onboarding efforts, improve accessibility for traders in emerging markets, and provide additional time for participants to prepare and qualify.

Participants already registered for the original July 2025 event may retain their entry or request a full refund by contacting support@worldtradingtournament.com.

In conjunction with this update, WTT is launching its official Co-Branded Mini Tournament Program. This initiative enables selected community leaders and trading influencers to host WTT-sponsored tournaments with a fully funded USD 350 prize pool. Each mini tournament will be coordinated with the WTT operations team and must meet specific participation criteria.

Co-Brand Mini Tournament Prize Pool

These Mini Tournaments will serve as a lead-up to the March 2026 Main Event, encouraging early participation and community involvement under WTT's brand and oversight.

WTT reaffirms its commitment to transparency, fairness, and innovation as it continues to build a structured, competitive global trading platform.

Media Contact:

World Trading Tournament Customer Support

support@worldtradingtournament.com

https://worldtradingtournament.com

Photos accompanying this announcement are available at

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/95160a63-101d-4e3f-8824-03ea0f94d855

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/2557ef84-0877-4b0c-b647-5b761da73ff5

www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/9b98b307-bf0a-4098-bdaa-7874841cad5d

World Trading Tournament: Dates Updated

World Trading Tournament: Dates Updated

Co-Brand Mini Tournament Prize Pool

data.title: Japanese Yen Holds Decline on Trade Concerns
data.description:
The Japanese yen traded near 145 per dollar on Friday after falling nearly 1% in the previous session, weighed down by trade-related uncertainties despite Tokyo’s efforts to secure a deal with Washington ahead of next week’s deadline.

US President Donald Trump announced plans to begin sending formal letters on trade tariffs as early as today, potentially signaling new tariff rates or extensions of existing deadlines.

He has previously threatened to raise tariffs on Japanese goods to as much as 35%, citing Japan’s limited imports of American rice and automobiles.

Adding to the yen’s weakness, the US dollar strengthened following a stronger-than-expected June jobs report, which eased recession fears and reduced expectations of imminent rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

On the domestic front, data showed that household spending in Japan rebounded more than anticipated in May, supported by government measures to stimulate consumption.

data.title: ASX Shares Rise After Signing MoU With Fujitsu, Tokyo Stock Exchange to Develop ETF Platform
data.description:
Shares of ASX edged up in recent Friday trade after Fujitsu said in a late Thursday statement that the company signed a memorandum of understanding with the Japanese digital service provider and the Tokyo Stock Exchange (TSE) to co-develop a SaaS-based Request for Quote (RFQ) platform for the Australian exchange-traded fund market.

The new platform will be modelled on Connecqtor, an RFQ system built by TSE and Fujitsu, per the statement.

Fujitsu will handle service delivery and support, while TSE will support implementation as the licensor of the system.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

data.title: HSBC Buys Back Over 650,000 Shares From Morgan Stanley
data.description:
HSBC said it bought back for cancellation over 650,000 shares from Morgan Stanley, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the lender were little changed in morning trade Friday in Hong Kong.

The bank bought back 649,132 shares from UK avenues, which include the London Stock Exchange, Aquis Exchange, and Cboe Europe, at a volume weighted average price of 8.83 pounds each.

HSBC Holdings also repurchased a further 6,400 shares from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange at a volume weighted average price of HK$94.64 apiece.

The shares were repurchased as part of the company's buyback program.

data.title: Highfield Resources Advances Equity Subscription With Qinghai Salt Lake
data.description:
Highfield Resources shared progress in the proposed $300 million equity subscription by Qinghai Salt Lake, a China Minmetals subsidiary, with due diligence ongoing following exclusive rights granted under a letter of intent (LOI), according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

To facilitate the completion of due diligence and ongoing negotiations toward a binding agreement, Highfield, Qinghai Salt Lake, and Yankuang Energy Group are working to formally extend the LOI exclusivity period, the filing said.

Although progress has been made since the LOI was signed, due diligence and negotiations on the QSL cornerstone placement and Southey Vend-in are still underway, with no binding agreement reached, the filing added.

data.title: Gold Poised for Weekly Gain
data.description:
Gold edged up to around $3,330 per ounce on Friday, heading for a weekly advance as concerns over the US fiscal deficit and lingering tariff uncertainty boosted the metal’s safe-haven appeal.

On Thursday, the House approved President Donald Trump’s massive package of tax and spending cuts, which is expected to add more than $3 trillion to the country’s deficit over the next decade.

Meanwhile, Trump said he would begin sending letters to countries on Friday specifying what tariff rates they will face on imports to the US—a clear shift from earlier pledges to negotiate individual deals.

However, gold's upside was capped by strong US labor market data, which showed that firms added a more-than-expected 147,000 jobs in June and the jobless rate unexpectedly dropped to 4.1%, strengthening the case for the Federal Reserve to hold interest rates steady.

For the week, the precious metal is on track to post a gain of over 1%, ending two consecutive weeks of losses.

data.title: Marvel Gold Extends Completion Date for Mali Project Sale
data.description: Marvel Gold agreed with Anchises Capital to extend the completion date of the sale of its Tabakorole and Yanfolila gold projects in Mali to Aug. 5, citing ongoing delays at the Ministère des Mines du Mali, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

data.title: Australia's real estate stocks rise on RBA rate cut hopes
data.description:
** Rate-sensitive real estate stocks (.AXRE) rise as much as 1% to their highest since June 11

** Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) expected to cut interest rates on Tuesday, Reuters poll of economists shows

** Property developer Scentre Group up 0.8%; Goodman Group rises as much as 1.8% to its highest since February 18 on $2.7 billion HK Data Centre Partnership

** AXRE gains 6% this year, as of last close

data.title: Copper Flat Amid Concerns Over Waning Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Copper is flat in early Asian trade amid concerns over weakening demand. Copper prices have gained more than 16% to breach USD10,000 a ton since the U.S. announced its reciprocal tariffs on trading partners, ANZ research analysts say. However, interest from Chinese fabricators appears to be waning due to the elevated prices, they say in a commentary. The three-month LME copper contract is flat at $9,959.00 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Marimaca Copper Finds High-Grade Copper Mineralization at Chile Deposit
data.description:
Marimaca Copper found high-grade, sediment-hosted copper sulfide and oxide at Pampa Medina in Chile, expanding the deposit in all directions, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Drilling at the deposit targeted extensions of shallow oxide-chalcocite mineralization, revealing sediment-hosted copper mineralization, with further drilling intersections suggesting potential extensions to 1.4 kilometers by 1.2 kilometers, the filing said.

The company expanded its diamond drilling at the deposit and allocated a 14-hole follow-up program, according to the filing.

data.title: Anjoy Foods Opens Flat in Hong Kong Trading Debut
data.description:
Anjoy Foods Group made a flat trading debut in Hong Kong Friday morning as shares opened at the IPO price.

The Chinese quick-frozen food company opened at HK$60.00 per share, matching its IPO price.

data.title: South Korean Auto Stocks Appear Inexpensive — Market Talk
data.description: South Korean auto stocks appear to be trading at a discount with their fundamentals and opportunities in physical artificial intelligence seemingly overlooked, Daiwa Capital analysts Henny Jung and Yoonki Bae say in a note. The new Seoul government's pledge to invest 100 trillion won, equivalent to $73.34 billion, in AI and expand related infrastructure will likely accelerate the commercialization of autonomous driving and humanoid robots, where Hyundai Motor Group is already active through its affiliates, the analysts say. They choose the group's flagship carmaker Hyundai Motor and its auto-component-making affiliate Hyundai Mobis as their top picks in the local auto sector. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Asian Currencies May Face Renewed Volatility as Focus Shifts to Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: Asian currencies may face renewed volatility on a shift in focus to tariff-related developments, MUFG Bank's Lloyd Chan says in a research report. The risk of higher U.S. reciprocal tariffs looms, the senior currency analyst says. Notably, the Korean won, which is frequently used as a proxy hedge for global risk sentiment, fell 0.6% against the U.S. dollar in Thursday's session, Chan says. A breakthrough in U.S.-Korea trade talks appear to remain slim for now, and the South Korean government plans to ask for an extension of the tariff pause, Chan adds. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,366.30, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: India regulator bars Jane Street from accessing its securities market
data.description:
India's market regulator said it had barred global fund Jane Street from accessing the nation's securities market, after an investigation into alleged market manipulation.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) posted an order on its website dated July 3 outlining that Jane Street would no longer be able to participate in the domestic securities market.

Jane Street did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters.

SEBI said it would 'impound' 48.4 billion rupees ($566.71 million) which it said was the 'unlawful gains earned' from the alleged misconduct.

"Entities are restrained from accessing the securities market and are further prohibited from buying, selling or otherwise dealing in securities, directly or indirectly," the SEBI notice said, referring to Jane Street entities.

SEBI said Jane Street's activity on existing positions would be monitored until the regulator's investigation is finalised.

($1 = 85.4050 Indian rupees)

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
Market Talks covering the impact of U.S. Politics and White House policies on companies and markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2135 ET - Asian currencies may face renewed volatility on a shift in focus to tariff-related developments, MUFG Bank's Lloyd Chan says in a research report. The risk of higher U.S. reciprocal tariffs looms, the senior currency analyst says. Notably, the Korean won, which is frequently used as a proxy hedge for global risk sentiment, fell 0.6% against the U.S. dollar in Thursday's session, Chan says. A breakthrough in U.S.-Korea trade talks appear to remain slim for now, and the South Korean government plans to ask for an extension of the tariff pause, Chan adds. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,366.30, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1622 ET - Wireless companies are cheering the passage of Republicans' massive spending bill, which offers them new tax breaks and fresh chances to bid on federal spectrum auctions. The bill orders the U.S. to privatize an estimated $85 billion worth of wireless rights, opening them up for future sale to cellphone carriers like AT&T and T-Mobile. Republicans reached a compromise on the auction authority, which lapsed in 2023, after adding safeguards for some airwaves used by military and intelligence agencies. (andrew.fitzgerald@wsj.com; @drewfitzgerald)

1448 ET - The Trump administration's budget reconciliation bill aims to boost homebuilding, Benchmark analyst Reuben Garner says. The bill, which passed through the Senate and House, proposes a new, permanent 12% housing credit allocation increase, Garner says. It also aims to cut the bond test threshold in half to 25%, which is estimated to nearly double the projected number of affordable homes built in the next decade to around 1 million, Garner says. Overall housing supply remains well below pre-pandemic levels and there is a shortage of about 5.8 million houses nationwide, Garner says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1418 ET - President Trump is scheduled to speak at the Iowa State Fair tonight. Grain traders are placing their bets on what he might announce during the speech. "The bulls argue that Trump would not head to the heart of America to start the Independence Day celebration if he was not going to announce something substantial," AgResource says in a note. The firm adds that instituting a nationwide ethanol blending standard of E15 is also a possibility for tonight. Most-active corn is up 0.5% and soybeans are up 0.1% late in the session, while wheat is down 1.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1025 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper down 0.3% at $9,976 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 0.1% at $2,617.0 a ton. Copper prices have slid back after briefly breaching the $10,000 a ton mark earlier in the session. They remain up 2.6% on week on expectations of a U.S. import tariff and beliefs that Chinese smelters could be short on copper concentrate, SP Angel analysts say in a note. The market is awaiting the outcome of a U.S. investigation into copper imports, with flows of the metal being directed to the U.S. to get ahead of a potential tariff. This has put pressure on warehouses outside the U.S. and driven premiums on U.S. commodities exchange Comex up to $1,400 over LME prices, SP Angel says.(joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0948 ET - The likelihood of the House of Representatives passing the Big Beautiful Bill has grains higher in early trading. That's in part due to the bill's provision for biofuel production requiring North American feedstocks under the 45Z program. After the U.S. trade deal with Vietnam, grain traders are also speculating that other deals will soon be announced, says Doug Bergman of RCM Alternatives in a note. Most-active corn is up 1% in early trading, and soybeans rise 0.5%. Wheat traded higher premarket, but has turned lower, with that contract down 0.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0936 ET - The U.S.-Vietnam trade deal and its 20% import tariff are a negative for softline retail stocks as they increase the probability that more tariff increases are coming for other countries, UBS analysts say in a research note. They expect Wall Street CY25-26 EPS estimates will fall 3% to 5% for the average stock in that sector, with Victoria's Secret, G-III Apparel Group and Under Armour being among the most exposed stocks. The situation is worse for athletic footwear brands that won't have an easy time diversifying their sourcing away from Vietnam, the analysts say. Softline stocks rose on the deal, possibly because the market priced in a higher Vietnam tariff, thought more tariff increases aren't coming and believes tariff costs will be easy to mitigate, the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0927 ET - Uncertainty about how tariffs will impact inflation is likely to keep the Fed on hold, since employment remains robust. "The big picture remains the same," AllianceBernstein's Eric Winograd writes, adding that the labor market is "holding up even in an uncertain economic environment." He says the Fed could cut rates soon "if they wanted to" since inflation data has been mild, but it is more likely to wait until September "to be confident about the inflation side of things." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0920 ET - Nintendo's Switch 2, with manufacturing in Vietnam, will likely get pricier following a finalized trade deal between the U.S. and Vietnam, but any ensuing pullback in its stock will likely be a buying opportunity, Wedbush says in a research note. The new agreement with Vietnam imposes a 20% tariff on Vietnamese imports to the U.S. "We anticipate Nintendo will pass nearly all tariff-related costs to U.S. consumers, resulting in an estimated 18%-20% price increase," say the analysts. They add that pullbacks in Nintendo shares into the holiday period present a solid buying opportunity "as we think demand will continue to outpace supply through the balance of the year despite anticipated price hikes." (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0858 ET - Moody's Ratings lowers its global sovereign outlook to negative from stable, the ratings firm says in an outlook. The revision is based on uncertainty about trade policy and a potential overhaul of global trade, which hurt credit conditions for sovereigns globally, it says. Moody's Ratings' 2025 outlook previously assumed a gradual macro normalization and a pivot to longer-term reforms. However, policy uncertainty will divert attention to short-term support measures, it says. This will slow fiscal consolidation and constrain productivity-boosting investment, according to the ratings firm. Compared with the start of 2025, Moody's has lowered its growth forecast for all regions, with the largest downward revisions made for North America, APAC and MENA. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0814 ET - Bitcoin rises to a three-week high as crypto sentiment is buoyed by easing macroeconomic worries and continued exchange traded fund inflows, Saxo Bank analysts say in a note. A trade agreement between the U.S. and Vietnam boosted market optimism with tariffs set lower than feared, they say. Wednesday's softer ADP private payrolls report increased bets for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, further supporting risk appetite. Institutional demand for cryptocurrencies remains strong with BlackRock's bitcoin ETF and the new iShares ethereum ETF rising. Meanwhile, Ripple, which manages a dollar-backed stablecoin called RLUSD, has applied for a national banking license. It joins a number of cryptocurrency companies trying to cross into mainstream finance. Bitcoin rises to $110,279. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0738 ET - European investors are becoming more optimistic about U.S. stocks as concerns about trade tensions fade, Morningstar's Kenneth Lamont says in a note. Net inflows into European-domiciled U.S. equity funds increased in May and June, Lamont says. The shift in favor of U.S. stocks indicates a cautious rise in investor confidence ahead of the deadline for the U.S. tariffs pause on July 9, he says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2135 ET - Asian currencies may face renewed volatility on a shift in focus to tariff-related developments, MUFG Bank's Lloyd Chan says in a research report. The risk of higher U.S. reciprocal tariffs looms, the senior currency analyst says. Notably, the Korean won, which is frequently used as a proxy hedge for global risk sentiment, fell 0.6% against the U.S. dollar in Thursday's session, Chan says. A breakthrough in U.S.-Korea trade talks appear to remain slim for now, and the South Korean government plans to ask for an extension of the tariff pause, Chan adds. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,366.30, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2046 ET - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in the morning session amid mixed signals. On the one hand, the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report released overnight was much stronger than consensus, NAB's Tapas Strickland says in a commentary. On the other hand, however, pricing for Fed rate cuts was trimmed, with now 51.4bps worth of cuts for 2025 versus 65.0bps on Wednesday, the head of Market Economics says. "As for meeting timing, a September cut is priced at 18.1bps (or 72% priced) from 29.1bps on Wednesday," Strickland adds. USD/KRW is up 0.2% at 1,366.50, and AUD/USD is 0.1% higher to 0.6573, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2018 ET - JGBs edge lower in the morning Tokyo session, tracking overnight price declines in U.S. Treasurys. However, uncertainty over the BOJ rate-hike outlook has increased as the U.S.-Japan trade-talk deadline on July 9 approaches, two members of Barclays' FICC Research say. "If the deadline is reached without any significant progress and if tariff hikes or other measures are actually imposed, BOJ rate hike expectations could recede," the members say in a research report. This may lead to downward pressure on yields, mainly on the front end, and an increase in volatility and selling pressure in the super-long sector, they add. The 10-year JGB yield is up 1.5bps at 1.455%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2013 ET - Japanese stocks are higher in early trade after solid U.S. jobs data boost optimism over the U.S. economic outlook. Bank and electronics stocks are leading the gains. Mizuho Financial Group is up 1.7% and Advantest is 1.8% higher. USD/JPY is at 144.70, up from 143.89 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any developments related to U.S. tariffs as well as domestic policymaking ahead of the upper-house election later this month. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.3% at 39920.82. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2004 ET - The Reserve Bank of Australia has a case to cut interest rates next week, but it's likely to be cautious about the strength of the job market. Here, the outcomes are still strong, says Paul Bloxham, chief economist at HSBC. The unemployment rate has continued to track sideways, and it remains the case that wage growth is still running too quickly, given that productivity is falling, he adds. Still, Bloxham sees this as unlikely to stand in the way of a cut in July, but it should leave the RBA cautious. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

1941 ET - Japanese stocks may rise after solid U.S. jobs data boosts optimism over the U.S. economic outlook. A weaker yen may also support the market. Nikkei futures are up 0.7% at 39975 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 144.79, up from 143.89 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. tariffs as well as domestic policymaking ahead of the upper-house election later this month. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 39785.90 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1446 ET - Australia's household spending indicator for May at 0130 GMT will attract a lot of attention following recent retail activity indicators suggesting consumers are lacking the confidence needed to boost consumption. A weak HSI, which is a broader measure of spending than the monthly retail sales data, will further support market pricing for an interest rate cut next week. The RBA is expected to cut interest rates by further 25 basis points, but there's also an argument that policy makers may wait until August when they will have seen the latest quarterly CPI numbers. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

1448 ET - The Trump administration's budget reconciliation bill aims to boost homebuilding, Benchmark analyst Reuben Garner says. The bill, which passed through the Senate and House, proposes a new, permanent 12% housing credit allocation increase, Garner says. It also aims to cut the bond test threshold in half to 25%, which is estimated to nearly double the projected number of affordable homes built in the next decade to around 1 million, Garner says. Overall housing supply remains well below pre-pandemic levels and there is a shortage of about 5.8 million houses nationwide, Garner says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1435 ET - Robust labor indicators keep Treasury yields from a fourth consecutive weekly decline, as markets close early ahead of tomorrow's holiday. The dollar also takes a boost. Job creation in June was stronger than forecast and unemployment unexpectedly declined. Layoffs remain contained. Healthy employment could allow the Fed to stay on hold until the impact of tariffs on inflation becomes more evident, despite pressure from the Trump administration for monetary easing. A tax-and-spending bill expected to widen budget deficits advances in Congress. The WSJ Dollar Index rises 0.3%. The 10-year yield ends the week up 0.056 percentage point, at 4.339%. The two-year rises 0.142 p.p. this week, to 3.883%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1406 ET - Brazil's economic indicators have been mostly benign, while government spending remains a source of concern, UBS analysts write. They expect the central bank to be able to start trimming its steep, 15% benchmark interest rate later this year or in early 2026. UBS labels Brazil's floating-rate and inflation-indexed bonds attractive, while fixed-rate bonds are deemed less attractive, for local portfolios. UBS keeps a neutral view on Brazilian equities, but says flows from foreign investors have been robust and valuations are appealing. The prospect of falling borrowing costs in the near future supports "a constructive view" for stocks, the analysts say. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1205 ET - Gold futures fall following stronger-than-expected U.S. Nonfarm Payroll data. Futures are down 0.6% at $3,338.30 a troy ounce. The report showed 147,000 new jobs added in June, compared with 110,000 expected, alongside a decline in the unemployment rate. The much-stronger figure means a July interest rate cut should no longer be in consideration by the Federal Reserve, StoneX's Fawad Razaqzada says in a note. This has dented the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion. The report has boosted risk-on sentiment and the U.S. dollar, further weighing on gold's safe-haven aspects, Razaqzada says. Still, the data also points to lower wage gains and the composition of job additions--heavily tilted towards government hiring--mean the report is less inflationary than it appears at first glance, complicating the Fed's calculus, Razaqzada adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1122 ET - While Canada's goods-trade deficit with the world narrowed in May, the country's energy surplus narrowed to the lowest level in nearly four years, National Bank of Canada's Jocelyn Paquet notes. That was partly due to continued soft oil prices for the month, but also to a decline in export volumes, the economist says. Energy volumes have now fallen 9.2% since December, the largest five-month drop since the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: Malaysia Oil-and-Gas Sector's Prospects Look Mixed — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's oil-and-gas sector is expected to see steady upstream activity and strong midstream gas demand, MIDF says in a note. Liquefied natural gas infrastructure growth, rising power needs from data centers, and regional energy transition efforts should support midstream resilience, it says. However, weak petrochemical margins and potential oil oversupply remain key risks, it adds. While geopolitical tensions may lift short-term prices, MIDF maintains a neutral rating on the sector. It favors MISC for its diversified earnings and exposure to long-haul shipping and floating production storage and offloading vessel projects.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: KOSPI Dips as Investors Brace for Tariff Deadline
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI fell 0.7% to around 3,090 on Friday pulling back from gains in the previous session as investors grew cautious amid lingering uncertainty over the potential reimposition of tariffs ahead of the looming deadline.

In response, President Lee Jae Myung emphasized his administration’s ongoing diplomatic push, with Trade Minister Yeo set to make his second visit to Washington this weekend.

The visit aims to ramp up last-minute negotiations with US officials in a bid to secure an extension of the three-month tariff pause, which is set to expire on July 9.

Domestically, South Korean equities have been lifted by proposed Commercial Act reforms expanding board duties to all shareholders.

The government is also reviewing dividend tax changes to boost payouts and attract more foreign investment.

On the corporate front, losses were led by autos and chipmakers such as LG Energy Solution (-1.41%), Hyundai Motor (-0.35%), Samsung Electronics (-0.31%), and SK Hynix (-0.18%).

data.title: Australia Personal Spending Grows the Most in 7 Months
data.description:
Household spending in Australia rose by 0.9% month-over-month in May 2025, picking up from a marginally revised flat reading in the previous month and marking the fastest monthly growth since October 2024.

On an annual basis, expenditure climbed 4.2%, up from a 3.8% increase in April, representing the strongest yearly gain in five months.

data.title: Hong Kong Private Sector Shrinks at Fastest Pace Since March 2022, S&P Says
data.description:
Hong Kong's private sector experienced its steepest deterioration in business conditions since March 2022, as new orders and output fell more sharply in June, S&P Global said on Thursday.

The S&P Global Hong Kong SAR Purchasing Manager's Index fell to 47.8 in June from 49.0 in May, marking the fifth consecutive month of contraction.

Private sector firms cited weaker demand across both local and international markets, with new order inflows seeing the most pronounced decline since March 2022. Business sentiment also weakened further, leading to cuts in purchasing activity and stagnant employment.

Output declined solidly, the sharpest fall in a year, while new business from both mainland China and overseas dropped at faster rates than in May.

Although firms kept staffing levels unchanged, growing concerns over weak demand and global trade conditions led to increased caution in hiring. Business confidence fell to its lowest level since August 2020.

On prices, average input costs rose at the slowest pace since January 2021, and firms reduced selling prices for the third time in four months, often citing efforts to stimulate demand through discounting.

S&P Global's Usamah Bhatti said the June data "showed that business conditions worsened at the end of the first half of 2025," and warned that forward-looking indicators suggest the downturn could deepen in the second half of the year.

data.title: Yihai International Announces Investigation Against An Independent Non-Executive Director
data.description:
Yihai International Holding Ltd :

  • INVESTIGATION AGAINST AN INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

  • INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR UNDER INVESTIGATION FOR SUSPECTED SERIOUS VIOLATIONS OF DISCIPLINE AND LAW

data.description:
Australian Government Takeovers Panel:

  • ACCEPTED UNDERTAKING FROM NEW WORLD RESOURCES ON APPLICATION DATED 1 JULY BY KINTERRA CAPITAL GP CORP II

data.description:
Australia's ACCC:

  • CLEANAWAY ACQUISITION OF CONTRACT RESOURCES NOT OPPOSED

data.title: ASX Says Total New Capital Quoted A$11.0 Billion In June 2025
data.description:
ASX Ltd :

  • IN JUNE 2025, TOTAL NEW CAPITAL QUOTED WAS A$11.0 BILLION

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan's Spring Wage Hikes Hit Above 5% for Second Straight Year, But Small Firms Lag Behind
data.description:
Japanese companies agreed to raise wages by an average 5.25% in this year's spring wage negotiations, marking the second consecutive year of increases above 5%, The Mainichi reported Thursday, citing the country's largest labor union, Rengo.

The final tally from over 5,000 unions showed an average monthly wage hike of 16,356 yen or $110, following last year's breakthrough 5% increase - the first in 33 years, said the publication.

Despite the gains, wage growth continues to trail behind persistent inflation, leaving workers with limited real income growth, added the report.

Smaller firms lagged significantly, offering just 4.65% or 12,361 yen yen average raises, highlighting growing disparities between large and small businesses in Japan's labor market, said the news daily.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Argosy Minerals Poised for Growth as Lithium Market Shows Signs of a Rebound
data.description: Battery metals specialist Argosy Minerals has requested a trading halt ahead of the announcement of a much-anticipated capital raising. The proposed raising follows the company’s recent announcement of its execution of a 60-tonne lithium carbonate spot sales contract. Argosy will produce the battery quality (approximately 99.5% purity) lithium carbonate from its Rincon project in Argentina. Strong Support Expected Evolution Capital is assisting Argosy with the raising for a minimum of $2 million to fund further development works at Rincon. Strong support is expected for the placement, with the late June announcement of the deal with the unnamed Hong Kong-based chemical company leading to a spike in interest and enquiries from battery industry participants. Argosy has also reported that several parties had accessed its data room as the company pursues strategic partner opportunities. Particularly Attractive Argosy’s battery metals portfolio is considered particularly attractive with seemingly improved sentiment for lithium companies highlighting the potential broader outlook for the space. “We were pleased to receive such strong interest for the sale of our battery quality lithium carbonate product,” managing director Jerko Zuvela said in announcing the Hong Kong deal. “With our positive project fundamentals, we will be strong beneficiaries of the EV/lithium sector resurgence noting the significant development milestones achieved to date at our Rincon lithium project.” SMM Fixed Price The spot sales contract has an agreed fixed price referenced from Shanghai Metals Market (SMM) battery grade lithium carbonate price. Delivery terms include free on board at the port of Buenos Aires, with an upfront payment of 30% and the 70% balance paid prior to ship loading. “We are delighted to be part of an exclusive group of battery quality lithium carbonate product exporters, given the challenges encountered by many of our peers attempting to achieve this feat,” Mr Zuvela added.

data.title: WA Kaolin Posts Record Sales of AU$2 Million in June Quarter; Shares Up 8%
data.description:
WA Kaolin posted a record June quarter, with AU$2 million in sales on volume of 9,786 tonnes, up 84% from the previous best quarter, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company is ramping up production and expanding its workforce as it transitions to a 24-hour-a-day, six-days-a-week operation, positioning itself to exceed original output targets and meet rising sales demand, the filing said.

July orders currently stand at 5,562 tonnes, within a total forward order book of 12,911 tonnes as of July 1, the filing added.

The company's shares were up almost 8% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Torque Metals Starts drilling at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Torque Metals started about 6,500 meters of reverse circulation drilling at its Paris Gold project in Western Australia, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The fully funded AU$550,000 program will run for about a month, targeting multiple high-priority targets to expand known mineralisation, the filing said.

Up to 50% of drilling costs may be settled in equity under an existing drill-for-equity agreement, the filing added.

The company's shares gained over 3% in recent Friday trade.
